analysis.note: Correlative with tadā; the commentary glosses with yasmin kāle ... tasmin kāle.
External sandhi: tu + ālambanam > tv ālambanam.
Object of upalabhate; interpreted by the commentary as deśanālambana, avavādālambana, or ordinary rūpa-śabda-ādi-ālambana, not as a mere grammatical support.
Neuter nominative singular, the subject of upalabhate; the form is formally identical with accusative, but the commentary repeats jñānaṃ ... nopalabhate.
Part of the printed sandhi-complex naivopalabhate = na + eva + upalabhate.
With na, emphatic negative naiva, rendered “not at all.” Part of the same printed sandhi-complex naivopalabhate.
Ātmanepada 3 sg. kartari; construed transitively: jñānam apprehends ālambanam. The commentary glosses the negated action by na paśyati, na gṛhṇāti, na abhiniviśate.
Correlative of yadā; glossed by tasmin kāle in the commentary.
Kta-participle used predicatively with jñānam/cittam: “is established.” The commentary paraphrases with pratiṣṭhita/avasthita bhavati.
Locative singular of the abstract stem in -tva from vijñaptimātra; translated as the technical “representation-only,” and glossed by the commentary as svacittadharmatā, the own-nature of mind.

verification.detail: compound stem vijñaptimātratva as Napumsaka/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation karmadharaya: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Locative of the bhāṣya reading grāhya-ābhā-abhāva, “absence of the appearance of the graspable.” The commentary’s explanatory lemma shortens the point to grāhyābhāve/grāhya-abhāva: the inferential force is absence of the graspable/its appearance.

verification.detail: compound stem grāhyābhābhāva as Pum/Saptami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

analysis.note: Ablative of cause: “because of the non-grasping of that.” In the commentary’s quotation before iti it appears with voicing as tadagrahād.

verification.detail: compound stem tadagraha as Pum/Panchami/Eka: pass; relation tatpurusa: unsupported (logged)

justifications.decision: The commentary explicitly expands the pair with yasmin kāle and tasmin kāle, so I keep the temporal structure rather than making yadā causal or conditional in the main translation.
justifications.chosen: yadā ... tadā as a temporal correlative: “when ... then.”

evidence.quote:
yasmin kāle deśanālambanaṃ avavādālambanaṃ prākṛtaṃ vā rūpaśabdādyālambanaṃ
... tasmin kāle vijñānagrāhasya prahāṇaṃ
evidence.translation: “At the time when” there is such an object-support ... “at that time” there is abandonment of grasping cognition.

justifications.decision: I translate “cognition does not apprehend an object-support,” not “one does not apprehend cognition” or “cognition is the object.” The commentary repeats exactly this construal and supplies examples of ālambana.
options_considered: jñānam as nominative subject, ālambanam as accusative object
jñānam as accusative object with an unexpressed subject
vulgate vijñānam as the intended subject regardless of the bhāṣya reading
justifications.chosen: jñānam is the subject, and ālambanam is the apprehended object-support.

evidence.quote:
yasmin kāle deśanālambanaṃ avavādālambanaṃ prākṛtaṃ vā rūpaśabdādyālambanaṃ
jñānaṃ bahiś cittān nopalabhate
evidence.translation: At the time when cognition does not apprehend, outside mind, a teaching-support, an instruction-support, or an ordinary support such as form, sound, and so on.

justifications.decision: The translation uses “apprehend” and specifies “outside mind,” because the commentary glosses nopalabhate with seeing, grasping, and fixating, and places the denied object bahiś cittāt/cittān.
options_considered: fails to sense because of blindness or incapacity
justifications.chosen: upalabhate means apprehends/perceives/graspingly notices, not obtains or acquires.

evidence.quote: jñānaṃ bahiś cittān nopalabhate na paśyati na gṛhṇāti nābhiniviśate
evidence.translation: Cognition does not apprehend outside mind, does not see, does not grasp, does not fixate.
This is because of seeing reality as it is, not like someone blind from birth.

justifications.decision: I take sthitam as a predicative nominative neuter referring to cognition/mind, not as an adjective modifying ālambanam. The locative is not a physical location but the Yogācāra state/own-nature of mind.
options_considered: predicative: cognition/mind is established in representation-only
sthitam modifying ālambanam: an established object-support
locative as merely spatial: located in representation-only
justifications.chosen: sthitaṃ vijñaptimātratve means “is established in representation-only,” glossed as being established in the own-nature of mind.

evidence.quote:
tasmin kāle vijñānagrāhasya prahāṇaṃ
svacittadharmatāyāṃ ca pratiṣṭhito bhavati
evidence.translation: At that time there is abandonment of grasping cognition, and one/it is established in the own-nature of mind.

evidence.quote:
grāhyagrāhakābhiniveśānuśayāḥ prahīyante svacittadharmatāyāṃ ca cittam
avasthitaṃ bhavati
evidence.translation: The latent tendencies of fixation on grasped and grasper are abandoned, and mind becomes settled in the own-nature of mind.

justifications.decision: The apparatus does not silently replace the bhāṣya’s jñānam/vijñaptimātratve with the vulgate vijñānam/vijñānamātratve. The commentary’s own prose continues with jñāna-language and glosses the state as svacittadharmatā.
options_considered: non-technical MW sense of vijñapti as request/report
justifications.chosen: The bhāṣya reading jñānam and vijñaptimātratve is followed, with vijñaptimātratva rendered “representation-only.”

evidence.quote: yadā tv ālambanaṃ jñānaṃ naivopalabhate tadā | sthitaṃ vijñaptimātratve grāhyābhābhāve tadagrahāt
evidence.translation: When cognition does not apprehend an object-support, then it is established in representation-only, because of the non-grasping of that when the graspable-appearance is absent.
A nonconceptual supramundane cognition arises, with ālambya and ālambaka on the same footing.

justifications.decision: Morphologically I retain the supplied bhāṣya form grāhya-ābhā-abhāva, but I do not translate it as mere optical darkness or “lack of splendour.” The commentary explains the reason through the absence of grāhya and consequent absence of grāhaka.
options_considered: absence of the graspable/graspable-appearance entails non-grasping
ābhā as ordinary splendour/light, giving an optical reading
agraha as mere non-acceptance unrelated to grāhya/grāhaka
justifications.chosen: grāhyābhābhāve tadagrahāt is causal: when the graspable or its appearance is absent, there is no grasping of it and no grasper.

evidence.quote:
atraiva kāraṇam āha
grāhyābhāve tadagrahād iti
evidence.translation: He states the reason for just this: because, when the graspable is absent, there is non-grasping of that.

evidence.quote:
grāhye sati grāhako bhavati na tu grāhyābhāva iti
grāhyābhāve grāhakābhāvam
evidence.translation: When the graspable exists, there is a grasper, but not when there is absence of the graspable; in the absence of the graspable, there is absence of the grasper.

translation:
When cognition (jñāna), however, does not at all apprehend
an object-support (ālambana) outside mind,
then it stands established in representation-only (vijñaptimātratva):
for, when the graspable-appearance (grāhyābhā) is absent,
there is no grasping of that.
one_shot_delta: A commentary-blind translation would likely follow the vulgate and say “consciousness-only” (vijñānamātratva), missing the bhāṣya’s jñānam and vijñaptimātratve.
It might translate upalabhate as “obtains” or take ālambana as a generic support; Sthiramati glosses it as not seeing/grasping/fixating on an object-support outside mind.
It might make the non-apprehension a mere failure of perception, like blindness; Sthiramati says it occurs through yathābhūtārthadarśana, seeing reality as it is.
It might miss that sthitam is predicative of cognition/mind and that vijñaptimātratva is glossed as svacittadharmatā, the own-nature of mind.
It might read grāhyābhābhāve as an optical “absence of light/splendour”; Sthiramati explains the point as absence of the graspable and hence absence of grasper/grasping.
analyzer_disagreements: jñānam: ByT5’s jñāna_SANe was not retained as an accusative analysis; I construe it as Prathama Eka neuter, the subject of upalabhate, because trbh 1487 has jñānaṃ ... nopalabhate.
upalabhate: ByT5 gives the lexical unit upalabh; per the requested format I give bare root labh with prefix upa. The finite features are otherwise retained.
sthitam: ByT5 lemmatizes to sthā; I analyze the verse-form as the nominal kta-stem sthita, Prathama Eka neuter, predicative with jñānam/cittam.

vijñaptimātratve: ByT5 splits vijñapti_mātra_tve and treats tva as the inflected element; I analyze a single locative singular subanta, stem vijñaptimātratva, with -tva as an abstract suffix on vijñaptimātra.
grāhyābhābhāve: ByT5 splits grāhya_ābhā_abhāve; I analyze the supplied bhāṣya form as one locative tatpuruṣa compound, stem grāhyābhābhāva.
tadagrahāt: ByT5 splits tad + agrahāt; I analyze the printed verse-form as a single ablative singular tatpuruṣa, tadagraha.
open_questions: The supplied bhāṣya verse reads grāhyābhābhāve, while the explanatory lemma at trbh 1491 reads grāhyābhāve. I keep the supplied verse-form in the morphology and translate it through the commentary’s grāhya-absence explanation.
